title: SEC3: challenge closure leaked via forget() after auth completes
description: client.rs lines 40-62: The temporary challenge-capture closure is installed on the transport and then `forget()`-ed. After the challenge nonce is received and `wire_notify_callback` installs the permanent closure (overwriting the slot on `notify_cb`), the challenge closure is unreachable from Rust but JS still holds the allocation via the boxed FnMut. It fires at most once more (the `take()` makes subsequent calls no-ops) but the allocation is never freed. The fix is to NOT `forget()` the challenge closure, instead hold it alive in a local variable across the `challenge_rx.await`, then let it drop naturally when it goes out of scope after the nonce is received. `Closure::forget` should be reserved for closures that must outlive the stack frame; here the challenge closure has a well-defined end-of-life.
